title: The strange case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
author: Stevenson, Robert Louis
summary: Mr. Utterson, a sober London lawyer, hears from his kinsman Richard Enfield about a sinister, neglected door and a violent episode in which a small girl is trampled by a mysterious, repellent man named Hyde. Enfield recounts how Hyde paid off the child’s family with a cheque drawn on the highly respectable Coutts and led them to a secretive lodging. Utterson studies Dr. Henry Jekyll’s strange holograph will naming Edward Hyde as sole beneficiary and grows troubled. The narrative establishes mood, mystery, and moral unease as respectable society is linked to a repulsive figure and a baffling legal claim.
